title: asperula glomerata subsp. dasycarpa
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. dasycarpa, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, Woodruff has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Woodruff is often prepared as a tea or added to herbal infusions, and it can also be used fresh in salads or as a garnish, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
